diagrams.title: Digital Design Abstraction Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Pyramid showing six levels of abstraction from System at top to Physical at bottom
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate the top-down design approach in digital systems, showing how each level refines the previous one into more detailed implementation. This helps students understand how complex systems are designed through progressive refinement.

diagrams.title: Architecture vs Organization
diagrams.description: Venn diagram showing two overlapping circles with shared and unique regions
elements: Left circle: Computer Architecture (Instruction set, Registers, Addressing modes)
Right circle: Computer Organization (ALU, Cache, Bus design, Microarchitecture)
Center overlap: Both (registers visible to programmer, memory organization)
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the distinction between what a computer does (Architecture) and how it does it (Organization). This visual helps students understand these often-confused terms through clear categorization.

diagrams.title: Digital Signal vs Analog Signal
diagrams.description: Side-by-side waveform comparison showing continuous analog sine wave vs stepped digital representation
elements: Top: Smooth continuous curve - analog signal varying from 0 to 5V
Bottom: Stepped/staircase waveform with discrete levels at 0V and 5V - digital signal
Arrow showing sampling points on analog wave
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate the fundamental difference between analog (continuous) and digital (discrete) representations, helping students understand why digital systems use discrete voltage levels and how conversion between domains works.
